review_text: Iam writing this review and would like others to provide their experience. In Fortworth Texas I purchased 5 12pk Pepsi soft drinks at Kroger grocery store, there was a sale buy 2 get 3 12pks free. Upon drinking these Pepsi products we were disappointed to find them utterly stale and flat. Any others that noticed this problem?

review_title: Trying to kill Type 1 diabetics??
review_text: Pepsi have now changed their "full sugar" version of their product to have half as much sugar... since then it tastes awful.   Not to mention it is seriously misleading for anyone with Type 1 diabetes trying to escape a case of "hypoglycemia".  They should be forced to display the sneaky ingredients on the product.   It is a very deceptive  business practice.  I hope they go bust.  Cheeky pricks

review_title: We were faithful customers at our…
review_text: We were faithful customers at our restaurant in Bellevue, WA, which we closed on December 22, 2025.  Since then we have called Pepsi 800-963-2424 every week to have our deposit for the CO2 cylinders returned to us.  We have spent hours every time waiting for an answer, it is not the deposit amount of 9 cylinders anymore, it is the point that they make- to ignore their customers.

review_title: Pepsi is deliberately eliminating Orangina
review_text: Pepsi is engaged in a worldwide elimination of Orangina - it owns the US license but stopped selling it. Orangina is disappearing also from the stores in Europe, being replaced by Fanta, a product that, unlike Orangina, is entirely artificial. This is a typical huge company selfish interest crushing what is better for the customer, or at least annihilating customers' choices. I find this conduct to be sinister and cruel. I for one, will never buy their products, even if stuck in the middle of the desert with nothing else to drink. Now what one finds on the shelfs even in Europe is ONLY Fanta. Orangina is extinct. Pepsi is trying to compensate for its failure to compete with Coca Cola by leaving the buyer no choice but Fanta. As it will find out, coercion does not produce popularity but, deservedly, resistance and resentment.

review_title: I drink this every day
review_text: I drink this every day. Only the canned version tastes good.

review_title: A once great tasting drink now sadly…
review_text: A once great tasting drink now sadly gone downhill like so many well known brands have.  Honestly....its tasting more like a bottle of cough syrup now than a bottle of a sugary quencher of thirst.   Sad to see this happening to so many 'once great' products,  but this is the world that we live in now.  Sugar content dramatically lowered now and I understand why,  but now replaced by garbage ingredients and the result being a very poor quality end product desperately clinging on to its well known brand name.   To the marketeers and producers,  it's fine relying on the brand name providing that what is being supplied is still of reasonable quality.  Consumers are not stupid,  they know when a product has rapidly gone downhill in taste,   I wont be buying another bottle thats for sure.   Very poor excuse for a once reputable product.

review_title: Been Watered Down?!?!!
review_text: Been Watered Down?!?!!! There's no bite. No rich Pepsi syrup. It tastes crappy very much like Coke! You can't make soda healthy! People don't buy it for that resason! review_title: Awful sucrose taste in AN ORIGINAL…
review_text:
Awful sucrose taste in AN ORIGINAL PEPSI CAN. 
Tried to complain to PEPSI online but their process is conniving and make it impossible to submit a complaint because by the time you type why you've contacted them, when you go yo take photos of proof of purchase, your typing previously disappeared. 
Bought 24 cans case from Iceland, opened 1 can and the taste was like a diet version, opened 3 more cans and all the same. Now this is ORIGINAL PEPSI I bought. Waste of money and no recourse to do anything about it.
